title: Databricks Job
description: Schema representing a Databricks job, which is the primary mechanism for running automated workloads. Jobs can execute notebooks, JARs, Python scripts, Spark submit applications, SQL queries, dbt tasks, and Delta Live Tables pipelines. Multi-task jobs support complex DAG workflows with task dependencies, scheduling, and monitoring capabilities.

job_id.description: The canonical identifier for this job, assigned by Databricks upon creation.

creator_user_name.description: The email address of the user who created this job.

run_as_user_name.description: The email address of the user whose credentials are used to run this job. Defaults to the creator.

created_time.description: The time the job was created, represented as epoch milliseconds.

settings.description: The configuration settings for this job.

name.description: The name of the job. This does not need to be unique.

tags.description: Tags associated with the job for organization and filtering.

tasks.description: The list of task specifications for a multi-task job. Each task defines a unit of work with its own compute configuration, dependencies, and task type.

job_clusters.description: A list of job cluster specifications that can be shared and reused by multiple tasks in this job, reducing startup time and configuration duplication.

no_alert_for_skipped_runs.description: If true, no notifications are sent for skipped runs.

no_alert_for_canceled_runs.description: If true, no notifications are sent for canceled runs.

timeout_seconds.description: The maximum allowed total execution time for the job in seconds. If exceeded, the job is set to a TIMED_OUT life cycle state. A value of 0 means no timeout.

continuous.description: Settings for a continuously running job. When configured, the job automatically restarts after each run completes, suitable for streaming workloads.

pause_status.description: Whether the continuous execution is currently paused.

trigger.description: Trigger-based execution settings, such as file arrival triggers.

url.description: The cloud storage URL to monitor for new file arrivals.

min_time_between_triggers_seconds.description: Minimum time in seconds between trigger evaluations.

wait_after_last_change_seconds.description: Time to wait after the last file change before triggering.

max_concurrent_runs.description: The maximum number of concurrent runs allowed for this job. Setting to 1 ensures runs execute sequentially.

format.description: The format of the job, indicating whether it is a single-task or multi-task job.

queue.description: Queue settings. When enabled, jobs are queued when max_concurrent_runs is reached instead of being skipped.

enabled.description: Whether queuing is enabled for this job.

parameters.description: Job-level parameter definitions with default values that can be overridden at run time.

default.description: The default value for this parameter.

run_as.description: The identity to run the job as. Can be a user or service principal.

user_name.description: The email of the user to run the job as.

service_principal_name.description: The application ID of the service principal to run the job as.

run_history.description: Summary of recent run history for this job.

TaskSettings.description: Configuration for a single task within a job.

task_key.description: A unique key for this task within the job. Used to define dependencies between tasks.

description.description: A human-readable description of this task.

depends_on.description: The tasks that must complete before this task can run, forming the DAG structure.

outcome.description: The expected outcome of the dependency task.

existing_cluster_id.description: The ID of an existing all-purpose cluster to run this task on.

new_cluster.description: Specification for a new job cluster to create for this task. See the Databricks Cluster schema for the full specification.

job_cluster_key.description: Reference to a job_clusters entry defined at the job level.

notebook_path.description: The absolute path of the notebook in the Databricks workspace or relative path from the Git root.

source.description: The source of the notebook (workspace or Git repository).

base_parameters.description: Base parameters passed to the notebook. Can be overridden at run time.

main_class_name.description: The full name of the class containing the main method to execute.

parameters.description: Parameters passed to the main method.

jar_uri.description: Deprecated. Use libraries to specify the JAR.

spark_python_task.description: Configuration for a Python script task.

python_file.description: The URI of the Python file to be executed.

parameters.description: Command-line parameters passed to the Python file.

spark_submit_task.description: Configuration for a Spark submit task.

pipeline_task.description: Configuration for a Delta Live Tables pipeline task.

pipeline_id.description: The ID of the Delta Live Tables pipeline to execute.

full_refresh.description: Whether to run a full refresh of the pipeline.

python_wheel_task.description: Configuration for a Python wheel task.

package_name.description: The name of the Python wheel package to execute.

entry_point.description: The named entry point to run within the package.

query_id.description: The ID of the saved SQL query to execute.

dashboard_id.description: The ID of the SQL dashboard to refresh.

alert_id.description: The ID of the SQL alert to evaluate.

warehouse_id.description: The ID of the SQL warehouse to execute the task on.

project_directory.description: The relative path of the dbt project directory.

schema.description: The target schema for the dbt project.

warehouse_id.description: The SQL warehouse ID for dbt execution.

catalog.description: The Unity Catalog name for dbt.

profiles_directory.description: The relative path to the dbt profiles directory.

run_if.description: The condition under which this task should run, based on the outcomes of its dependencies.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum allowed execution time for this individual task in seconds.

max_retries.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for a failed task.

min_retry_interval_millis.description: Minimum interval in milliseconds between retry attempts.

retry_on_timeout.description: Whether to retry the task when it times out.

libraries.description: Libraries to install on the cluster before running this task.

JobCluster.description: A reusable cluster definition for job tasks.

job_cluster_key.description: A unique key for this job cluster. Referenced by tasks using job_cluster_key.

new_cluster.description: The cluster specification. See the Databricks Cluster schema for the full specification.

CronSchedule.description: A cron-based schedule for the job.

quartz_cron_expression.description: A Quartz cron expression describing the schedule. See Quartz documentation for syntax details.

timezone_id.description: A Java timezone ID. The schedule is resolved relative to this timezone.

pause_status.description: Whether the schedule is currently paused.

GitSource.description: Git repository source configuration for version-controlled job code.

git_url.description: The URL of the Git repository.

git_branch.description: The branch to use. Mutually exclusive with git_tag and git_commit.

git_tag.description: The tag to use. Mutually exclusive with git_branch and git_commit.

git_commit.description: The commit hash to use. Mutually exclusive with git_branch and git_tag.

EmailNotifications.description: Email notification settings for job or task lifecycle events.

on_start.description: Email addresses to notify when a run begins.

on_success.description: Email addresses to notify when a run completes successfully.

on_failure.description: Email addresses to notify when a run fails.

on_duration_warning_threshold_exceeded.description: Email addresses to notify when run duration exceeds the threshold.

no_alert_for_skipped_runs.description: If true, notifications are suppressed for skipped runs.

WebhookNotifications.description: Webhook notification settings for job or task lifecycle events.

id.description: The ID of the webhook notification destination.

Library.description: A library to install on the cluster. Exactly one of the fields must be specified.

jar.description: URI of a JAR library to install.

egg.description: URI of a Python egg to install (deprecated).

whl.description: URI of a Python wheel to install.

package.description: The package name with optional version specifier.

cran.description: CRAN package specification for R libraries.

requirements.description: Path to a requirements.txt file for Python dependencies.

RunSummary.description: Summary information about a job run.

run_id.description: The canonical identifier of the run.

result_state.description: The result state of the run.
